text: APIs allow different software applications to communicate with each other. When working with an API, you make HTTP requests, such as GET to retrieve data or POST to create new information. The API usually returns data in JSON format, which is easy for computers to read. Most APIs require authentication, often using an API key, to control access and ensure security. You should also be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a specific period. Finally, always implement error handling in your code. APIs may return error codes if something goes wrong, and your program should handle these gracefully to avoid crashes.
